issue.title: Aside recommended articles hidden on Reach PLC sites

labels.name: T: Incorrect Blocking
labels.description: Website or app not working properly due to filtering

labels.name: A: Cannot reproduce
labels.description: Filters devs cannot reproduce this issue

issue.body:
I found this problem from https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/pull/230853#discussion_r3207728418 On article pages for four Reach PLC sites[^sites], the aside section contains both recommended articles and ad units. When AdGuard is active, **all article cards in the aside are hidden**, leaving an empty sidebar. [^sites]: UK news publisher: https://www.reachplc.com/about-us/our-brands ### Affected sites - www.birminghammail.co.uk - www.mirror.co.uk - www.liverpoolecho.co.uk - www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk ### Suspected rules The aside rules in `BaseFilter/sections/specific.txt` (around [lines 5419–5425](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/b671a94378b904207a3a9c52e576273b86a00ec0/BaseFilter/sections/specific.txt#L5419-L5425)) are likely hiding editorial content alongside ad containers: ```adblock birminghammail.co.uk,...##aside span > span.react-loading-skeleton birminghammail.co.uk,...##aside > div[class^="sc-"]:has(> span > span.react-loading-skeleton) birminghammail.co.uk,...##section[data-testid^="commercial-"] ``` - The `sc-` prefixed styled-components selector with `:has(react-loading-skeleton)` - may apply before content hydrates by React, permanently hiding article card containers. - The `[data-testid^="commercial-"]` prefix may also match editorial sections.

issue.body:
I found this problem from https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/pull/230853#discussion_r3207728418 On article pages for four Reach PLC sites[^sites], the aside section contains both recommended articles and ad units. When AdGuard is active, **all article cards in the aside are hidden**, leaving an empty sidebar. [^sites]: UK news publisher: https://www.reachplc.com/about-us/our-brands ### Affected sites - www.birminghammail.co.uk - www.mirror.co.uk - www.liverpoolecho.co.uk - www.manchestereveningnews.co.uk ### Suspected rules The aside rules in `BaseFilter/sections/specific.txt` (around [lines 5419–5425](https://github.com/AdguardTeam/AdguardFilters/blob/b671a94378b904207a3a9c52e576273b86a00ec0/BaseFilter/sections/specific.txt#L5419-L5425)) are likely hiding editorial content alongside ad containers: ```adblock birminghammail.co.uk,...##aside span > span.react-loading-skeleton birminghammail.co.uk,...##aside > div[class^="sc-"]:has(> span > span.react-loading-skeleton) birminghammail.co.uk,...##section[data-testid^="commercial-"] ``` - The `sc-` prefixed styled-components selector with `:has(react-loading-skeleton)` - may apply before content hydrates by React, permanently hiding article card containers. - The `[data-testid^="commercial-"]` prefix may also match editorial sections.
Screenshots without AdGuard
Screenshots with AdGuard active
### Expected behavior Ad units in the aside are hidden. Recommended article cards remain visible. ### Notes Need a selector that distinguishes ad-only containers from mixed editorial+ad containers, or a more specific `data-testid` value.
